title: Income Tax Department Clarifies Transition to New Income Tax Act, 2025
content:
# Income Tax Department Clarifies Transition to New Income Tax Act, 2025

Ek important update hai! The Income Tax Department has announced crucial clarifications regarding the transition to the new Income Tax Act, 2025, which replaced the Income Tax Act, 1961, from April 1, 2026. This official announcement, made on May 18, 2026, aims to provide clarity on compliance during this transitional phase.

Here are the key takeaways from the department's update:

*   **Applicability of Old Act:** The provisions of the Income Tax Act, 1961, will continue to govern all tax years that commenced before April 1, 2026.
*   **E-filing Portal Support:** The Income Tax Department's e-filing portal (incometax.gov.in) is designed to support compliance under both the old and new Acts concurrently.
*   **Assessment Year 2026-27 Returns:** Taxpayers filing returns for Assessment Year 2026-27 (which pertains to income earned under the old Act) in July 2026 will use forms prescribed under the Income Tax Act, 1961.
*   **Advance Tax under New Act:** Advance tax payments for the Tax Year 2026-27, commencing from June 2026, will strictly adhere to the provisions of the new Income Tax Act, 2025.
*   **Pending Applications:** Any pending applications related to tax years before April 1, 2026, will be processed under the Income Tax Act, 1961.
*   **Validity of Old Circulars:** Existing circulars, instructions, and notifications issued under the 1961 Act will remain valid unless they are in conflict with the new Act.
*   **New "Tax Year" Concept:** For income earned from Financial Year 2026-27 onwards, the concept of "Tax Year" will replace the erstwhile "previous year" and "Assessment Year."

This clarification ensures a smooth transition for taxpayers, making sure there's no confusion regarding which provisions apply during this changeover period.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/)

*Published on: 19 May 2026*
